LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFF JAVIER AVELLANEDA'S MOTION FOR LEAVE TO FILE FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT IS DENIED. PLAINTIFF SEEKS TO ADD MR. MARTINEZ AS A NEW PLAINTIFF WITH DISTINCT FACTS. PREJUDICE WILL RESULT TO DEFENDANT BY THE ADDITION OF A NEW PLAINTIFF LESS THAN THREE WEEKS BEFORE TRIAL AND AFTER THE CLOSE OF DISCOVERY. FURTHER, PLAINTIFF CONCEDES THAT MR. MARTINEZ MAY FILE A SEPARATE ACTION FOR HIS CLAIMS. THE MOTION IS ALSO DENIED TO THE EXTENT THAT PLAINTIFF, IN HIS REPLY BRIEF, SEEKS TO ADD TWO NEW CAUSES OF ACTION. THIS GROUND FOR AMENDMENT WAS NOT RAISED IN THE MOVING PAPERS, WHICH, TO THE CONTRARY, EXPLICITLY STATED THAT PLAINTIFF WAS NOT ADDING ANY NEW CAUSES OF ACTION IN THE FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT. (MPA AT 3, LINES 8-9.) ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: MARLA J. MILLER, CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; COURT REPORTER: ANTHONY C. VAUGHN, CSR # 6185 =302/MJM

RULING - A R G U E D; The Court adopted its tentative ruling. PLAINTIFF JAVIER AVELLANEDA'S Motion For Leave To File First Amended Complaint is Denied without prejudice to renewing the motion before the trial judge. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: A. James Robertson II, Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Court Reporter: Anthony C. Vaughn, CSR # 6185 =(302/AJR)

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Leave To File First Amended Complaint
Off Calendar Feb-21-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. R. MICHAEL FLYNN OF THE FLYNN LAW OFFICE, TEL. NO. 989-8000 EXT. 24 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
ATTY. MICHAEL D. McCONATHY OF THE LYNCH GILARDI & GRUMMER LAW FIRM, TEL. NO. 397-2800 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANTS.

RULING - A R G U E D; The Court adopted its tentative ruling. PLAINTIFF JAVIER AVELLANEDA'S Motion For Leave To File First Amended Complaint is Denied. Plaintiff seeks to add Mr. Martinez as a new plaintiff with distinct facts. Prejudice will result to Defendant by the addition of a new plaintiff less than three weeks before trial and after the close of discovery. Further, Plaintiff concedes that Mr. Martinez may file a separate action for his claims. The motion is also denied to the extent that Plaintiff, in his reply brief, seeks to add two new causes of action. This ground for amendment was not raised in the moving papers, which, to the contrary, explicitly stated that Plaintiff was not adding any new causes of action in the First Amended Complaint. (MPA at 3, lines 8-9.) ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Marla J. Miller, Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Court Reporter: Anthony C. Vaughn, CSR # 6185 =302/MJM

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
